name: Old-Fashioned Baked Beans
recipeInstructions:
1. Wash the beans and discard any imperfect ones. Cover with 2 quarts cold water; refrigerate, covered, overnight.
2. The next day, transfer the beans and water into a 6-quart kettle.
3. Bring to boiling; reduce heat and simmer, covered for 30 minutes. Drain, reserving liquid.
4. Preheat oven to 300 degrees.
5. Trim the rind from the salt pork. Cut the pork at half-inch intervals.
6. Place the onion in the bottom of a 4-quart bean pot or casserole. Add the beans; bury the pork, cut side down, in the center of the beans.
7. Heat the reserved bean liquid to boiling.
8. Mix the remaining ingredients. Stir into the boiling bean liquid. Pour over the beans. Add boiling liquid just to cover the beans—about 1 1/2 cups.
9. Bake covered for 6 hours, stirring once every hour to ensure even cooking. If the beans seem dry after stirring, add a little boiling water.
10. To brown the top of the beans, remove the cover for the last half hour of baking time.
